report_name: Singapore Land Authority Breach Exposes Data of 70,000 via IBM-Managed System

threat_actors.name: Unidentified Actor
threat_actors.description: The unauthorized actor who gained access to a cloud-based development and testing environment managed by IBM, which contained sensitive data belonging to the Singapore Land Authority.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Supply Chain
attack_patterns.description: The data breach occurred due to a supply chain compromise, where an unauthorized actor gained access to a system managed by the third-party vendor, IBM.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor accessed and potentially exfiltrated a dataset containing real personal information from a cloud-based development and testing environment.

identities.name: Singapore Land Authority
identities.description: A Singaporean government agency that suffered a data breach exposing the personal information of approximately 70,000 individuals.

identities.name: IBM
identities.description: The third-party technology partner that managed the compromised cloud-based development and testing environment for the Singapore Land Authority.

identities.name: Government Technology Agency
identities.description: A Singaporean government agency involved in the investigation of the data breach.

identities.name: Cyber Security Agency of Singapore
identities.description: Singapore's national agency for cybersecurity, involved in the investigation of the data breach.

infrastructure.name: SLA Cloud Development and Testing Environment
infrastructure.description: A cloud-based environment managed by IBM for testing two of SLA's property registration systems. It was compromised by an unauthorized actor.

indicators.name: Anomalous Cloud Audit Log Activity
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Cloud Access by Source IP
id: 2b8a1c9e-7d4f-4b1a-9c8d-3e2f1a0b9c8d
status: experimental
description: Detects cloud access from IP addresses not associated with known corporate or developer locations, which could indicate compromised credentials.
references:
  - https://www.computerweekly.com/news/366645414/Breach-of-IBM-managed-environment-exposes-personal-data-of-70000-in-Singapore
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-03
tags:
  - attack.t1078.004
level: medium
logsource:
  product: azure
  service: signinlogs
detection:
  selection:
    ResultType: 0
    Status.errorCode: 0
  filter:
    IpAddress:
      - '1.2.3.0/24' # Placeholder for known corporate IP ranges
      - '4.5.6.0/24' # Placeholder for known developer home IP ranges
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects anomalous cloud provider log activity, such as access from unknown IP addresses or unusual API calls, which could indicate a breach of a cloud environment.

relationships.target_name: SLA Cloud Development and Testing Environment
